stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Quick loan in pipeline as Druzhba reopens
stories.summary: Russian oil can flow again, says Ukrainian president, leaving ‘no grounds’ for blocking €90bn EU package; warning of all-out Kremlin cyberwar. What we know on day 1,519 The Druzhba pipeline carrying Russian oil to Hungary and Slovakia is ready to resume operations , Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Tuesday, after Ukraine repaired the damage from a Russian attack. Kyiv now expects the EU to unlock a €90bn EU loan after Hungary’s prime minister Viktor Orbán, spent months blocking it. Orbán is about to

stories.title: Costa steps up pressure to break EU budget deadlock
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — European Council President António Costa is pressing EU leaders to make progress on the bloc’s next €1.8 trillion budget at a summit in Cyprus on Friday. As Europe wrestles with how to bring down energy prices and shore up its economy amid conflict in the Middle East, leaders arriving in Nicosia are unlikely to have the finer points of the EU’s next long-term budget at the top of their minds. But Costa is seeking a deal by the end of the year — and has carved out two hours for di

stories.title: Magyar wants to put the Austro-Hungarian Empire back on the map
stories.summary: Hungary’s next leader wants to revive Central Europe’s clout by tapping its imperial past. Prime Minister-elect Péter Magyar says he will deepen ties with neighboring states, especially Austria, building on strong economic links and a shared history rooted in the Austro-Hungarian Empire of the late nineteenth century. “We used to share a country, and Austria is a key economic partner of Hungary,” Magyar said after his victory over Viktor Orbán in the Hungarian election earlier this month. “I wou

stories.title: Nine lessons for the US from Viktor Orbán’s defeat | Kenneth Roth
stories.summary: As US elections approach, the Hungarian prime minister’s loss is a reminder that history does not march relentlessly toward autocracy Viktor Orbán’s electoral loss was a slap in the face for Donald Trump and JD Vance, who had enthusiastically endorsed Europe’s most visible autocrat but proved unable to salvage his candidacy. But Hungarian voters’ 12 April rejection of Orbán also holds important lessons for Americans who hope to resist Trump’s own autocratic tendencies. As the November midterm el

stories.title: Albanians are being unfairly demonised in illegal immigration debate | Letter
stories.summary: Albania’s ambassador to the UK, Uran Ferizi, takes exception to media reports that he says selectively focus on the criminality of Albanians • Report: Albanians in UK scapegoated by rightwing media and politicians, says ambassador These are challenging times in Britain. Economic pressure has a long history of finding a foreign face to blame. For several years now, that face has been Albanian – caricatured by certain media outlets and politicians in ways that are neither fair nor, frankly, very&n

stories.title: Albanians in UK scapegoated by rightwing media and politicians, says ambassador
stories.summary: In a letter to the Guardian , Uran Ferizi criticises ‘obsession’ with demonising Albanians Albanians in Britain are paying the price in schools and workplaces of being scapegoated by rightwing media and politicians, the Albanian ambassador has said. Uran Ferizi also criticised Shabana Mahmood, the home secretary, for comments in parliament where she singled out Albanians when discussing problems with immigration. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
stories.summary: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead

stories.summary: A multibillion euro aid package from the EU had been blocked over access to the pipeline, which brings Russian oil to countries like Hungary and Slovakia.

stories.title: A bútorok élni kezdtek, a szomszédból boszorkány lett – ilyen volt a világ első LSD-tripje
stories.summary: Albert Hofmann svájci vegyész látomásai több órán át tartottak, időnként olyan érzése volt, mintha a pokolba került volna. Hat órával a szer bevétele után tért vissza a valóságba.

stories.title: Ők osztották a pénzt, azt hitték, mindenkivel jóban vannak, aztán az egész megyét elvesztették
stories.summary: Bács-Kiskun a rendszerváltás óta az egyik leginkább jobboldali megye volt, négy éve a Fidesz még minden körzetben tönkreverte az ellenzéket, most viszont a Tisza mindenhol fordítani tudott. A megyében működött a Tisza nemzeti retorikája, és olyanok dolgoztak tömegesen a Tiszának, akik korábban soha nem politizáltak. Ennek meg is lett az eredménye.

This year will mark 45 years since an Iberia commercial flight from New York landed in Madrid with its pilot announcing to the surprised passengers that they had just travelled with one of the country’s most famous exiles: Pablo Picasso’s Guernica. stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
stories.summary: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

stories.title: Trump extends Iran ceasefire, blockade amid uncertainty over peace negotiations
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that he will not go forward with new strikes on Iran at the request of Pakistani officials, extending the ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran. Trump said Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir asked the U.S. to “hold our Attack on the Country of Iran until such time as [Iranian] leaders and representatives can come up with a unified proposal” to end the war. stories.title: UK intelligence: 100 nations have spyware that can hack Britain
stories.summary: GLASGOW — More than half of the world’s nation states are believed to have purchased technology that could be capable of hacking into Britain’s infrastructure, companies and private networks, U.K. intelligence has found. The U.K. National Cyber Security Centre — which is part of the GCHQ intelligence agency — believes around 100 countries have procured cyber intrusion software, suggesting the barrier for states to get their hands on the technology is dropping, the agency told POLITIC

stories.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
stories.summary: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Swedish intelligence said Russia has been covering up a weak, debt-driven economy hit by war costs and sanctions, warning of rising inflation and banking risks.

stories.title: Lufthansa axes 20,000 ‘unprofitable’ flights to save jet fuel
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Lufthansa is ending 20,000 short-haul routes across its network through October to achieve “jet fuel savings of more than 40,000 metric tons,” the airline announced on Tuesday. Jet fuel costs have “doubled since the outbreak of the Iran conflict,” Lufthansa said. The move follows last week’s decision to retire the entire 27-aircraft fleet of its subsidiary CityLine ahead of schedule amid surging fuel costs and tightening supply. stories.title: The lunchbox dilemma: Why Europe’s packaging push risks backfiring
stories.summary: As the Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation (PPWR) moves closer to application, all attention is on the deadlines for its practical delivery. Few provisions illustrate this more clearly than Articles 32 and 33, which seek to expand reusable packaging and ‘bring your own’ (BYO) options for take-away food and beverages across the EU’s hotel, restaurant and cafés (HORECA) sector. stories.title: Virginia voters approve new congressional maps in blow to Trump

stories.title: Pollen season in UK and mainland Europe extended by climate breakdown
stories.summary: Research finds global heating has already lengthened the pollen season in addition to worsening heatwaves and droughts Climate breakdown has extended the pollen season in the UK and mainland Europe by between one and two weeks since the 1990s, a study has found, adding itchy eyes and runny noses to the harm wrought by fossil fuel pollution. stories.title: ‘An element of exploitation’: the world of TikTok child skincare influencers
stories.summary: Experts say regulation of child influencers sits in a legal grey area as children promote products on social media #ToddlerSkincare: the ‘dark and exploitative’ world of children’s beauty videos on TikTok In a TikTok video a young girl – her age anywhere between 10 and 15 – sits unboxing package after package of products she says were sent to her by skincare brands. She calls it a “PR haul”. In another video, a 16-year-old opens a box of products she received from a well known brand. She says: “

stories.title: Sam Neill says New Zealand goldmine supporters have threatened him with violence
stories.summary: Actor, who has publicly objected to plans to fast-track project near his farm, says he has received personal abuse The actor Sam Neill says he has received threats of violence from supporters of a controversial goldmine that could be opened several kilometres away from his farm in New Zealand’s Central Otago district, after he publicly objected to the government’s plans to fast-track the mine. The Australian mining company Santana Minerals is pushing to expedite a 85-hectare (210-acre) open-cast

stories.title: ‘We were terrified they were going to kill us’: fishers who survived US boat strike speak out
stories.summary: An Ecuadorian fishing crew describe their ordeal as victims of Trump’s purported war on ‘narcoterrorists’ By 4pm, the light was softening over the Pacific, and the crew of the Don Maca were finishing a long day hauling in lines of swordfish and albacore. Down in the hold, the mood had settled into the familiar rhythm of a fishing day drawing to a close. “We were just working, waiting for the last trawler to return,” said Jhonny Sebastián Palacios, one of the fishers. “Everything was perfectly fi

stories.title: Kremlin forcing big firms to join ‘witch-hunt’ against internet rebels, claims report
stories.summary: Campaigners say banks and web platforms are being told to collect data on customers visiting blocked sites Major Russian companies have been conscripted into a “witch-hunt” against users trying to circumvent online controls, researchers have said, as the Kremlin continues trying to cut its citizens off from the global internet. Banks and web platforms are collecting data on users of virtual private networks (VPN) tools, which obscure an individual’s real location and allow them to access sites b

stories.title: Student allegedly jailed in China for six years after taking part in pro-democracy protests in Australia
stories.summary: Exclusive: The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect overseas students from political repression The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect Chinese international students from political repression by authorities on their return after a Chinese student was allegedly sentenced to six years’ imprisonment for joining pro-democracy protests in Australia. The student, who the Guardian has chosen not to name, lost contact with his frien

stories.title: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te abandons visit after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoke overflight permission Taiwan’s president, Lai Ching-te, has cancelled his trip to Eswatini, the democratic island’s only diplomatic ally in Africa, after his government said several countries had revoked overflight permits because of “intense pressure” from China. Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran war: Trump says US extending ceasefire to give Iran more time to negotiate
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he was ​extending the Iran ​ceasefire until an Iranian ⁠proposal ​is ​submitted, ​and discussions ‌are concluded. A US ceasefire with Iran was due to expire on Wednesday evening.

stories.title: Mexican pyramid gunman had materials tied to Columbine shooting, officials say
stories.summary: A 27-year-old suspect who opened fire at the Teotihuacan archaeological site possessed material related to the Columbine school shooting in the United States, Mexican authorities said.

stories.title: US in talks to send Afghan refugees to DR Congo
stories.summary: Afghan allies who assisted US forces now face a stark choice: resettle in the war‑torn Democratic Republic of Congo or return to Taliban‑ruled Afghanistan.

stories.title: Virginia voters approve plan that could boost Democrats' seats in Congress
stories.summary: The move could increase the Democrats' chances of winning four currently Republican-held seats in Congress in midterm elections in November.

stories.title: Australia targets Roblox, Minecraft, Fortnite and Steam in child safety push
stories.summary: Roblox, Minecraft, Fortnite and Steam were among the gaming services asked by authorities to explain how they are protecting children from sexual predators and radicalization.

stories.title: India news: West Bengal, Tamil Nadu gear up for voting day
stories.summary: After an intense campaign season, Tamil Nadu and West Bengal are hours away from going to the polls. Meanwhile, tributes and messages pour in on the first anniversary of the Pahalgam attack. Follow live.

stories.title: Digital memory at stake: Why news outlets block the Wayback Machine
stories.summary: The "Wayback Machine," custodian of digital memory, is fighting for its survival. An increasing number of media outlets are refusing to allow the Web Archive to archive their content.

stories.title: Record number of immigrants reside in EU in 2025
stories.summary: The foreign‑born population of the EU grew to around 64 million in 2025. Germany remains the biggest host of immigrants in absolute terms, but some smaller nations have a much higher percentage of foreign-born residents.

stories.title: What course will Bulgaria’s new leader Rumen Radev take?
stories.summary: The trained pilot, former general and ex-president says he will crack down on corruption and seek dialogue with Russia. But who is Rumen Radev, the man who swept to victory in Sunday’s election?

stories.title: Nigeria's $2B energy bailout tests President Tinubu reforms
stories.summary: Nigeria has approved billions to clear power sector debts. Analysts say the move may stabilize electricity supply, but deeper structural problems remain unresolved.

stories.title: UK ambassador to UAE warns of regional risk but backs UAE resilience amid fragile Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: In an interview with Euronews, British Ambassador to the United Arab Emirates Edward Hobart said the fragile US-Iran ceasefire should pave the way to a lasting peace with "a clear way forward".

stories.title: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
stories.summary: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

stories.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.summary: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time

stories.title: Virginia approves redistricting, giving Democrats edge in midterms
stories.summary: The new maps in Virginia could make it easier for Democrats to take control of the US House of Representatives in November.

stories.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
stories.summary: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

stories.title: Domestic workers legally recognised in Indonesia after '22-year struggle'
stories.summary: The country is home to some 4.2 million domestic workers - of which almost 90% are women.

stories.title: Phone tracking shows how Colombian mercenaries backed Sudan's RSF - report
stories.summary: The Conflict Insights Group (CIG) says its research also shows the extent of UAE involvement.

stories.title: Central Asian leaders gather at first ever regional ecological summit to discuss water crisis
stories.summary: The Regional Ecological Summit kicks off today in Astana.

stories.title: Trump wants to extend Iran ceasefire, until negotiations conclude
stories.summary: The US president also wrote on Truth Social that the blockade of Iranian ports will continue until Tehran presents a "unified proposal".

stories.title: Russia and North Korea connect road bridge ahead of summer opening, Moscow says
stories.summary: Ties between both countries have surged amid Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, with Moscow and Pyongyang deepening economic, political, cultural and military links.

stories.title: Qatar backs ceasefire as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad
stories.summary: Qatar has urged diplomacy on Tuesday as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran talks, warning prolonged tensions risk wider global fallout, particularly if disruption in the Strait of Hormuz affects energy flows and markets.

stories.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.summary: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

stories.title: Israeli army jails two soldiers for smashing head of Jesus statue in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had said on Monday he was "stunned and saddened to learn that an IDF soldier damaged a Catholic religious icon in southern Lebanon."

stories.title: German police launch mass raids of 50 premises to smash Syrian trafficking ring
stories.summary: Police believe the ring operated by using genuine residency documents issued to Syrian refugees in Germany, which were sent to people in Syria with a similar appearance.

stories.title: Myanmar military regime widens sanitary towel ban, claiming rebels use them for first aid
stories.summary: Activists say clamp down on period products to target insurgents is gender-based violence and violates rights Myanmar’s military regime is expanding its ban on the distribution of period products, claiming they are being used to treat wounded resistance fighters, according to local activists. The south-east Asian country has been locked in civil war since 2021 , when the military usurped the democratic government and launched a violent crackdown on dissidents. Artillery fire, the burning of town

stories.title: Almost 43,000 migrants register in first three days of Spain's regularisation amnesty
stories.summary: Spain's position sharply differs from prevailing attitudes on immigration in Europe, where many governments have been trying to curb arrivals and step up deportations.

stories.title: George Ariyoshi, first US governor of Asian American descent, dies aged 100
stories.summary: Democrat led Hawaii from 1973 to 1986, coinciding with the party’s rise to power in the state George R Ariyoshi – Hawaii’s former governor and the nation’s first Asian American governor – has died at age 100. Ariyoshi, a Democrat who led the state from 1973 to 1986, died peacefully while surrounded by family on Sunday night, according to a statement Monday from the current governor, Josh Green. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK agriculture deal with EU will not remove all red tape, peers told
stories.summary: Lords told sales of Scottish shellfish among areas that may benefit – but agreement will not erase all paperwork Europe live – latest updates A new agriculture agreement with the EU will not wipe out all Brexit paperwork but might pave the way for sales of Scottish langoustines and oysters, the House of Lords has heard. The UK and EU are close to finalising a sanitary and phytosanitary (SPS) agreement to reduce Brexit trade barriers, and while it will have “modest” impact on the UK economy the a

stories.title: Carney names broad team to advise on tense US-Canada trade talks
stories.summary: Conservatives and former provincial premiers among those PM names to advisory committee on economic relations Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney , says his new advisory committee on economic relations with the United States will draw on the “best advice and the broadest perspectives” as the country braces for what many expect will be tense trade negotiations with its southern neighbour. The 24-member advisory committee, announced on Tuesday, shows the prime minister’s eagerness to reach across

stories.title: EU foreign ministers reject proposal to suspend association agreement with Israel
stories.summary: A part suspension was tabled by Ireland, Spain and Slovenia but did not receive enough backing from other member states The EU remains split on imposing sanctions on Israel, despite some member states criticising the country over the plight of Gaza and violence against Palestinians by Israeli settlers in the West Bank. Kaja Kallas, the EU foreign policy chief, said proposals for a part suspension of the EU-Israel association agreement remained on the table but required states to shift their posi

stories.title: Why cloud computing still runs on coal and gas
stories.summary: Renewable energy is increasingly being used to supply power-hungry data centers. As the sector swells, much of the electricity demand is being met by polluting fossil fuels.

stories.title: Apple switches CEO: What it means for the iPhone maker
stories.summary: Apple's top hardware engineer John Ternus will lead the $4 trillion iPhone maker, as Tim Cook steps back. Will Apple chase rivals for the smartest intelligence, or deliver the best personal AI experience?

stories.title: Taiwan blames pressure from China for nixed Africa trip
stories.summary: The Taiwanese government says Beijing used economic coercion to convince three African nations to revoke permission for President Lai Ching-te to traverse their airspace.

stories.title: India news: Delhi braces for first heatwave of the year
stories.summary: The India Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert with temperatures over 40 degrees predicted for the coming days. Meanwhile, reports suggest India and the US are on the verge of a trade deal. DW has more.

stories.title: Mars: NASA's Curiosity rover finds new organic matter in Gale Crater
stories.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover has identified more organic compounds as scientists investigate any potential ancient life signs on Mars. It was exploring the massive Gale Crater also believed to be a now-dry lake.

stories.title: What are Japan, South Korea learning from Hormuz disruption?
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz blockade has shaken Tokyo and Seoul, highlighting their dependence on maritime trade for essential goods including food and fuel.

stories.title: UK moves to ban smoking for everyone born after 2008
stories.summary: A draft law in the UK to create a "smoke-free generation" by banning smoking for anybody born after 2008 has cleared both houses of parliament. Only the king's signature remains for it to become law.

stories.title: How grievance turns to violence in a mass shooter's mind
stories.summary: Attacks like last week's school shooting in Turkey can seem to come out of nowhere, but they rarely do. Instead, a buildup of grievance and fixation often precedes the the moment violent thoughts become violent acts.

stories.title: Lebanon war puts pregnant women at risk
stories.summary: Lebanon’s healthcare system is under severe strain despite a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. Thousands of displaced pregnant women are struggling to access medical services.

stories.title: Timelapse footage shows maritime traffic around Gulf, Straight of Hormuz
stories.summary: The passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz - the world's busiest oil shipping canal - has been severely disrupted amid the US-Israeli war with Iran. Timelapse footage shows maritime traffic in the Gulf and Strait of Hormuz from April 18 to April 20, amid a fragile US-Iran truce.

stories.title: Iran hangs man accused of ties to Israel and setting mosque on fire during pre-war protests
stories.summary: Iran executes Amirali Mirjafari for allegedly plotting to burn a Tehran mosque and collaborating with Israel and the US, as rights groups decry rising executions.

stories.title: The Onion launches new effort to turn Infowars into parody website
stories.summary: The new proposal would involve the publication licensing Alex Jones's company, which faces liquidation.

stories.title: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism
stories.summary: This clears the way for Japan to sell weapons to more than a dozen countries.

stories.title: Madonna offers reward for return of missing Coachella costume
stories.summary: The items, which she says are "part of my history", disappeared after her slot with Sabrina Carpenter.

stories.title: Dozens of Turkish coal miners detained after march for unpaid wages
stories.summary: Some 110 Doruk Madencilik workers who had been marching to Ankara to demand unpaid wages and compensation were detained as demands for rights and safety grow louder.

stories.title: Arrests in Nigeria after abduction of local monarch 'held for ransom'
stories.summary: The kidnappers raided his palace on Saturday and are believed to be holding him in a forest.

stories.title: Gaza needs more than $71 billion for recovery and reconstruction, EU-UN report says
stories.summary: Gaza would need over $71 billion for recovery after two years of war, EU and UN warned Monday, as damage to homes, hospitals, and schools leaves most people displaced.

stories.title: How will Apple change under 'product guy' John Ternus?
stories.summary: How Ternus responds to challenges facing Apple will go a long way to deciding the tech giant's future, writes Zoe Kleinman.

stories.title: Ofcom investigating Telegram over child sexual abuse material concerns
stories.summary: The popular messaging service told the BBC in a statement it "categorically denies Ofcom's accusations".

stories.title: Canadian killed in shooting at Mexico's ancient Teotihuacán pyramids
stories.summary: Several people were also injured in the incident on Monday at the popular tourist site, say Mexican officials, weeks before the country hosts the Fifa World Cup.

stories.title: Humpback whale Timmy remains stranded in shallow waters in northern Germany
stories.summary: A stranded humpback whale in the Baltic Sea remains stuck near Poel island, Germany, as rescuers struggle to guide it back to deeper waters.

stories.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.summary: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.title: South Africa's police boss charged in connection with controversial health contract
stories.summary: Fannie Masemola is accused of failing in his duties to provide proper oversight in his role.

stories.title: Zelensky says failure of US envoys to visit Kyiv is 'disrespectful'
stories.summary: US envoy Steve Witkoff and Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner have travelled to Moscow several times, but never to Kyiv.

stories.title: Mexico's president urges tighter security at tourist sites ahead of World Cup after shooting
stories.summary: Security officials said that the shooting at the world-famous Teotihuacan pyramids that left one person dead and 13 others wounded "wasn't spontaneous."

stories.title: Six stand trial in Istanbul over insecticide poisoning deaths of Turkish-German family
stories.summary: The Turkish-German Böcek family were staying at the Harbour Suites Old City hotel in Istanbul’s Fatih district when they fell ill on 12 November, the state-run Anadolu news agency said.

stories.title: Role of US officials killed in crash in Mexico under scrutiny
stories.summary: Two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

stories.title: Mass trial for 486 alleged gang members begins in El Salvador
stories.summary: Footage released by the attorney general's office shows large groups of men in prison attending the trial via video link.

stories.title: Almost 8,000 people died or disappeared on migration routes in 2025, IOM says
stories.summary: The migration agency highlighted that at least around 340,000 family members are estimated to have been directly affected by the missing migrant crisis since 2014.

stories.title: Ennél élethűbb asztali szerepjátékot nem nagyon csináltak még
stories.summary: Az Esoteric Ebb méltó utóda a 2019-es Disco Elysiumnak. Még úgy is, hogy sok helyen inkább hasonlít egy klasszikus Dungeons & Dragons-kampányra. Gyanús, hogy ez lesz az év indie játéka.

stories.title: Trump meghosszabbítja a tűzszünetet Irán és az Egyesült Államok között
stories.summary: Órákon belül lejárt volna, de a végleges megállapodás egyelőre nem körvonalazódik. Amerikai vezetők a Fehér Házban tanácskoznak a stratégiáról.

stories.title: A segélykiáltásokat követve egy vérző fejű nőt találtak a sziklás ösvény szélén, a rajta térdeplő férje egy kővel ütötte a fejét
stories.summary: Egy hawaii orvost bűnösnek mondott ki az esküdtszék. A vád szerint előre kitervelte, hogy lelöki egy szikláról a feleségét, majd amikor ez nem sikerült, egy kővel verte fejbe a nőt. A védelem szerint az elvált házaspár narratívái csaptak össze a tárgyaláson, de valójában ennél árnyaltabb a bűnügy.

stories.summary: A volt nyomozó korábban azt állította, titkosszolgálati nyomásra indult házkutatás a Tisza Pártot segítő informatikusok ellen.

stories.title: Olaszország bekérette az orosz nagykövetet, miután az orosz tévében idiótának, árulónak és prostituáltnak nevezték Giorgia Melonit
stories.summary: Vlagyimir Szolovjov, a Kreml legismertebb propagandistája többek között azzal támadta az olasz kormányfőt, hogy elárulta Trumpot, „akinek korábban hűséget esküdött”.

stories.title: Idén megindulhat az érdemi GDP-növekedés, jövőre visszatérhet a 400 forint körüli euró
stories.summary: Az Egyensúly Intézet gazdasági előrejelzése szerint a növekedés elsődleges motorja továbbra is a lakossági fogyasztás lesz, idén megindulhatnak a beruházások is.

stories.summary: Schmidt Gábor előbb az elnökség, majd a közgyűlés előtt méretteti meg magát, miután a szövetségen belülről nyilvános kritikát kapott.

stories.summary: A szervezet azzal védekezik, hogy valójában informátorokról van szó, akiket adatszerzés céljából fizettek, az eredményeket pedig a rendőrséggel is megosztották.

stories.title: Akár 30 százalékkal is drágulhatnak az óvszerek az iráni háború miatt
stories.summary: Ráadásul válság idején a kereslet is megnövekedett, hiszen ilyenkor az emberek nagyobb arányban igyekeznek elkerülni a gyerekvállalást.

stories.title: Arrests fuel fears among Madagascar’s gen Z protesters that new regime no better than one they overthrew
stories.summary: Jubilation is turning to disenchantment as young activists arrested after protest calling for election date to be set The arrest of several protesters in Madagascar has increased fears among young people that the military regime that took power last year after huge Gen Z demonstrations will be no better than the government it overthrew. stories.title: Iran talks on hold because of Trump’s blockade
stories.summary: Iran is refusing to send its negotiating team to Islamabad to continue talks this week until President Donald Trump lifts the U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, according to two Middle East officials briefed on the situation,granted anonymity because they were not authorized to speak publicly. Trump, who hinted on Tuesday that high-level negotiations with Iran would resume “soon,” has given mixed signals about when, or whether, Vice President JD Vance would leave the White House for Pakistan

stories.title: WWII bomb destroyed in controlled explosion in Paris
stories.summary: Video provided by French police shows the controlled detonation of a large bomb dating from the Second World War in Colombes. After attempts to remove the bomb’s detonator failed at midday, authorities buried it and later destroyed it in a controlled detonation.

stories.title: Charlize Theron joins chorus of disapproval over Timothée Chalamet’s ballet comments
stories.summary: The former ballet dancer said Chalamet’s comments were ‘reckless’ in an interview with the New York Times in which she also discussed her violent childhood Actor and former ballet dancer Charlize Theron has joined the chorus of disapproval aimed at Timothée Chalamet over his remarks that appeared to disrespect performers of ballet and opera . In an interview with the New York Times , Theron said: “Oh, boy, I hope I run into him one day,” adding: “That was a very reckless comment on two art forms

stories.title: Pakistan seeks to raise its global standing in push for Middle East peace
stories.summary: Islamabad has seized chance to act as mediator in Iran war and hopes to reap diplomatic and economic benefits As Pakistan works frantically to narrow differences between Iran and the US in its newfound role as global peacemaker, it is also seeking to recast its diplomatic standing and attract business. stories.title: Dagad az iráni konfliktus, kemény áremelést jelentett be a világ legnagyobb óvszergyártója
stories.summary: Az óvszerek iránti kereslet az idén mintegy 30 százalékkal emelkedett, mivel sokan nem tartanak otthon eleget.

stories.title: Kifizették Nagy Mártonék a Fertő tavi, füstbe ment kiruccanás költségeit
stories.summary: A Transparency International adatigénylést nyújtott be az elvonulást szervező Államadósság Kezelő Központhoz, így a pontos költségek is kiderültek.

stories.title: Állásfoglalást adtak ki a kutatóintézetek főigazgatói, szerintük módosítani kell a HUN-REN-törvényt
stories.summary: Az új kormánynak a szervezet jövőképét, a működési alapelveit újragondolásra javasolják, és tisztáznák az MTA-ingatlanvagyon alkotmánysértő helyzetének megoldását.

stories.title: Legalább 600 milliárdnyi pluszkiadást már idénre bevállalna az új kormány

stories.title: Az anyádban! – kiabálta felfelé a mozgólépcsőn Takács Péter arra a kérdésre, hogy „Hol a nyolcmillárd?”
stories.summary: A búcsúzó egészségügyi államtitkár szerint a lélegeztetőgépekről szóló ügy „akkora kamu, mint a gödi akkugyár”, és jogi lépéseket fog tenni.

stories.title: Kaja Kallas szerint 24 órán belül döntés születhet az Ukrajnának szánt, 90 milliárd eurós hitel átutalásáról
stories.summary: Az időtáv épp egybevág azzal, amin belül a Barátság kőolajvezetéken is megindulhat a szállítás.

stories.title: Janikovszky Éva nem alattvalókat nevelt, hanem egy szemmagasságban beszélt a gyerekekhez
stories.summary: Miért működnek még ma is a 100 éve született Janikovszky Éva-szövegek? A Buksó legújabb adásában az írónő generációkon átívelő humoráról, politikai üldözöttségéről és összetett irodalmi örökségéről beszélgetett Lovász Andrea, a Móra Könyvkiadó főszerkesztője és Janikovszky János, a Móra tulajdonosa, az írónő fia Nyáry Krisztiánnal.

stories.title: A miniszter arra sem méltatott, hogy válaszoljon a felterjesztésemre, hogy legyen Janikovszky Éva emlékév
stories.summary: Szabó Magdával való barátsága, a Réber-Janikovszky kettős által teremtett vizuális stílus és Janikovszky Éva rendszer általi megítélése is szóba került a Buksó legújabb adásában.

stories.title: Nem a hab a lényeg: milyen az eredeti Rákóczi-túrós, és milyen a legjobb?
stories.summary: Nem az a fontos, hogy ki és mikor találta fel, honnan ered a neve ennek a csodálatos süteménynek, hanem hogy melyik eleme a meghatározó. Egy habmentes verziót mutatunk, amelyben a túróé a főszerep, de a habnak is megvan a helye.

stories.title: Senkit nem hallgatott ki egy év alatt az MNB-ügy kecskeméti szálában a rendőrség, az ügyészség most magához veszi a nyomozást
stories.summary: Az egyetem alapítványa 127,5 milliárdot adott kölcsön a PADME vagyonkezelőjének súlyosan összeférhetetlen módon, a pénzt aztán elherdálták.

stories.title: Megvan az új parlament alakuló ülésének időpontja
stories.summary: Sulyok Tamás köztársasági elnök a Facebookon jelentette be: május kilencedikén, 10 órakor lesz.

stories.title: Belső lázadó, keménykezű vezető, jó szakember a Tisza lehetséges oktatási minisztere, akit szétszedett az internet
stories.summary: Sokan felháborodtak, hogy Rubovszky Rita lehet az oktatási miniszter. Vannak, akik szerint katolikus gyökerei és egyházi iskolai kötődése nem egyeztethető össze a világi oktatással. Az őt ismerők szerint viszont van egy fontos képessége, ami az oktatás megújításához kellhet: jobb irányba tud terelni rossz mentális állapotban lévő, rossz beidegződésű közösségeket.

stories.title: Borbás Marcsi azt mondja, 15 éve nincs tévéje, ezért nem tudta, mi megy a köztévén a műsorai előtt és után
stories.summary: Borbás a Partizán stúdiójában azt mondta, perre megy Kocsis Máté árulózása miatt.

stories.title: BBC: Pendrive-ra másolt dokumentumokkal kuncsorognak büntetlenségért a Tiszánál minisztériumi dolgozók
stories.summary: Az adatokért cserébe az állásuk megtartását kérik, illetve mentességet a büntetőjogi felelősségük alól.

stories.title: Üzent a brit énekesnő, akinek a száma táncra perdítette Hegedűs Zsoltot
stories.summary: A brit énekesnő egyszer csak azt vette észre, hogy ezrek jelölik meg a videójukban és tömegével kommentelnek évekkel ezelőtti száma alá.

stories.title: Bulgaria: Pro-Russia Rumen Radev wins ‌election
stories.summary: Rumen Radev's new center-left party, Progressive Bulgaria has won an absolute majority in the country's eighth general election in five years. Both Russia and the EU have already sent their congratulations.

stories.title: China's chip ambitions shake up global tech industry
stories.summary: US curbs on advanced chips pushed China to focus on its own semiconductor ecosystem. And while it trails at the very cutting edge, China's "good‑enough" technology is fast powering much of the global economy.

stories.title: Bulgaria enters uncharted territory as Radev wins big
stories.summary: Preliminary results show that former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgaria's general election with a clear majority and the biggest result for a single party since 1997. Will Bulgaria finally get a stable government?

stories.title: Bundesliga debut by female coach marred by misogyny
stories.summary: Marie-Louise Eta's historic debut as a head coach in the men's Bundesliga ended in defeat. While her appointment is a sign of progress, the reactions to it leave no doubt about how much is still to be done.

stories.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
stories.summary: Russia's return to the international contemporary art exhibition, as well as the participation of Israel and the US, have sparked politicized backlash.

stories.title: UK: Starmer says he would't have appointed Mandelson as US envoy if he'd known he failed vetting process
stories.summary: Under-fire Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the House of Commons that neither he nor his ministers were told that the former ambassador to the US and Epstein associate Peter Mandelson had failed his security vetting.

stories.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, John Ternus to take over
stories.summary: John Ternus, who has long focused ​on design and ‌products at Apple, will lead the iPhone maker as it gears up for an industry change spurred by artificial intelligence.

stories.title: Community holds vigil after Louisiana mass shooting
stories.summary: Residents gather near the scene of a mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana hugging and consoling one another after a father shot dead eight children in a domestic rampage.

stories.title: Japan issues tsunami warning after powerful 7.4 magnitude earthquake
stories.summary: Authorities have urged residents of the affected areas to seek higher ground, heed safety instructions and exercise caution.

stories.title: Turkey rallies global middle powers in Antalya amid Hormuz crisis
stories.summary: At the Antalya summit, Erdoğan brought together leaders of 150 countries to tackle crises, promote alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz, and showcase the growing diplomatic clout of middle powers.

stories.title: Are insider traders making millions from the Iran war?
stories.summary: The BBC has found significant spikes in activity shortly before the US president made some announcements.

stories.title: Rescuers evacuate hundreds after snowstorm hits Russia’s Far East
stories.summary: Dozens of vehicles were rescued from deep snowdrifts in Zabaikalsky Krai, where over 250 people were taken to shelters after a rare April snowstorm.

stories.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
stories.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

stories.title: Netanyahu condemns IDF soldier for vandalising Jesus statue in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned the desecration of a statue of Jesus Christ that was carried out by an IDF soldier in a southern Lebanese village.

stories.title: Victory slips away as marathon runner celebrates too soon
stories.summary: The dramatic end to the Delaware Marathon occurred when the lead runner slowed in celebration before a trailing marathoner sprinted toward the finish line .

stories.title: Turkey arrests 90 people with suspected links to so-called Islamic State group
stories.summary: Authorities have not officially connected the arrests to a shootout outside the Israeli consulate in Istanbul on 7 April in which two police officers were wounded.

stories.title: FBI director Kash Patel files $250m defamation suit against The Atlantic
stories.summary: According to The Atlantic, Patel's job as head of the Federal Bureau of Investigation is in jeopardy in part because of what it said were "bouts of excessive drinking" and "unexplained absences".

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV holds a giant open air Mass in Angola
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people attended a giant open-air mass by Pope Leo XIV outside the Angolan capital.

stories.title: Trump refuses to lift Hormuz blockade until Iran deal agreed
stories.summary: Trump’s announcement comes after Iran said earlier it would not be sending a delegation to participate in the second round of high-stakes peace talks in Islamabad that were set to take place on Monday.

stories.title: Blue Origin rocket grounded after satellite 'mishap'
stories.summary: The firm founded by Amazon billionaire Jeff Bezos is investigating the failed launch.

stories.title: Gunman opens fire on tourists at Teotihuacan pyramids in Mexico, killing one
stories.summary: Mexican authorities launched an investigation to uncover the motives behind the attack, which took place just before noon local time.

stories.title: Hot air balloon makes emergency landing in California backyard
stories.summary: "Look at that, wow!" - A couple in Temecula woke up to find a balloon boarded with 13 people behind their home on Saturday.

stories.title: Indian politicians are campaigning while holding fish. What is going on?
stories.summary: In West Bengal polls, fish moves from plate to politics, symbolising identity and cultural pride.

stories.title: Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after lifting tsunami warning
stories.summary: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week.

stories.title: US Labour Secretary Chavez-DeRemer quits amid misconduct probe
stories.summary: Us labour secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer quits Trump cabinet amid misconduct probes, including alleged affair with a subordinate and misuse of staff.

stories.title: Mexico City: Video shows armed man shooting at tourists at Teotihuacán pyramids
stories.summary: Video and photos published by local media shows a man standing with a gun on top of a pyramid while people duck for cover. A number of gunshots ring out in the videos.

stories.title: Several killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages – video report
stories.summary: Ukrainian investigators are examining whether a terrorist attack in Kyiv was directed by Moscow, after a man shot dead six people on Saturday before he was killed by police. The gunman, 58, was a Ukrainian citizen born in Moscow. He opened fire on passersby before barricading himself in a supermarket and taking hostages. Detectives sealed off the area in the Holosiivskyi district and tried to negotiate with him. He was killed after a 40-minute standoff Investigators examine whether Ukraine terro

stories.title: German crime figures: Are migrants unfairly targeted?
stories.summary: Is it true that the crime rate in Germany is higher among immigrants? Statistics suggest as much, but numbers by themselves can be misleading. An expert explains what's behind them.

stories.title: Germany News: Neighbor attacked for pounding schnitzel too loudly
stories.summary: Police in southern Germany were called in to settle a schnitzel dispute that had turned violent, with a man reportedly slapping his neighbor for pounding his schnitzel too loudly.

stories.title: US military strike on alleged drug boat kills at least three people in the Caribbean Sea
stories.summary: US strikes on drug boats in the Caribbean Sea and Pacific Ocean have so far killed a minimum of 181 people. Critics have slammed the Trump administration over the strikes, saying they constitute a violation of international law.

stories.title: UK seeks EU deals on steel and EVs in push for closer economic ties
stories.summary: Agreements would aim to shield British industry from new steel tariffs and stricter rules on electric vehicles due in 2027 Downing Street hopes to secure deals on steel and electric cars with the EU as it seeks to upgrade the post-Brexit economic relationship. Amid economic uncertainty caused by the conflict in the Middle East and strains in relations with the US, Keir Starmer is seeking closer economic ties with the EU. Continue reading...

stories.title: LensCulture portrait awards 2026 – in pictures
stories.summary: Winning and shortlisted works move seamlessly between documentary and invention. Across these images, tender couples hold each other close, people reclaim their identities from the burden of colonial memories and the harsh realities of the war in Ukraine come sharply into focus Australian photographer wins at world photography awards with ‘barefoot volcanologist’ image Continue reading...
